question.text: An insider makes a profit of Rs. 6 crore through illegal insider trading. The penalty under Section 15G could range from:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15G prescribes a minimum of Rs. 10 lakh and maximum of Rs. 25 crore or 3 times the profit, whichever is higher. Here, 3 × Rs. 6 crore = Rs. 18 crore. Comparing: Rs. 25 crore > Rs. 18 crore, so the maximum is Rs. 25 crore. The range is Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 25 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 5 lakh minimum applies to Section 15HA (fraudulent trade practices), not 15G (insider trading). Rs. 18 crore (3x profit) is not the maximum since Rs. 25 crore cap is higher in this case. **Cross-Exam Context:** Penalty calculations are tested in NISM Series IX, XII, and XV exams. Understanding the interplay between fixed caps and profit multipliers is essential. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 3x profits clause only becomes the binding maximum when profits exceed Rs. 8.33 crore. At Rs. 6 crore profit, the Rs. 25 crore cap is the effective maximum.
